citations.claim: The coordinator runs as the central routing and trust layer in the Darkbloom network, accepting provider WebSocket connections and routing OpenAI-compatible HTTP requests
Main function initializes core components including structured logging, database connections, provider registry, and configuration from environment variables
Server struct contains registry, store, billing service, rate limiters, and comprehensive middleware for authentication and observability
Routes function defines comprehensive REST API including consumer inference endpoints, provider WebSocket, billing, and admin interfaces
Registry manages provider fleet with hardware capabilities, trust levels, and round-robin routing among idle providers serving requested models
Consumer API implements OpenAI-compatible endpoints with streaming support, provider failover, and comprehensive timeout handling
Billing service provides unified payment processing via Stripe with support for deposits, Connect Express payouts, and referral rewards
Store interface defines comprehensive persistence layer for API keys, usage tracking, balance ledgers, provider records, and billing sessions
Protocol package defines WebSocket message types for coordinator-provider communication including registration, heartbeat, and inference flows
Payment ledger implements double-entry bookkeeping with micro-USD precision for consumer charges and provider payouts including platform fees
Provider WebSocket handler manages the complete provider lifecycle from registration through attestation verification and inference serving
Telemetry system ingests operational events with field allowlisting and rate limiting, forwarding to Datadog for monitoring
External dependencies include WebSocket library, PostgreSQL driver, cryptographic libraries, JWT validation, UUID generation, rate limiting, and Datadog integration
Datadog integration provides APM tracing, DogStatsD metrics, and Logs API forwarding for comprehensive observability
Billing configuration supports Stripe Checkout, Connect Express, referral system, and mock mode for development
Privy authentication integration handles JWT validation and device authorization flows for consumer accounts
HTTP server configuration includes proper timeouts for streaming inference with graceful shutdown handling
Consumer API implements comprehensive timeout handling with 10-minute inference timeout and first chunk timeout for provider failover
Provider trust levels range from none (no attestation) through self-signed to hardware (MDA + SE key verification)
Model catalog seeding function ensures consistent model availability across coordinator restarts with automatic cleanup of retired models
